steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: In similar triangles, corresponding angles are equal
steps.content: When two triangles are similar, their corresponding angles have equal measures. We're told that triangles LMN and PQR are similar with L corresponding to P and M corresponding to Q. This correspondence tells us which angles must be equal.

steps.title: Use the correspondence to find angle Q
steps.content: Since M corresponds to Q, angle Q must equal angle M. We're given that angle M = $53^{\circ}$, so angle Q = $53^{\circ}$. B is correct.
